title: LSH Format: AI-Native Data Format with Rust/Burn Engineering Validation
description: We present LSH Format, a compact text format designed for AI-native multi-modal interactions with 96% token savings compared to JSON. LSH Format uses semantic IDs (category_timestamp_hash), abbreviated attribute keys, and incremental update protocol. Benchmarks show 6-19x encoding/decoding speedup over JSON: for 100,000 elements × 3 attributes, LSH encodes in 10.4ms vs JSON's 194.3ms (18.6x speedup). We implement LSH Format in Rust with Burn deep learning framework for memory optimization, achieving 50%显存 reduction through static memory allocation, batch processing, mixed precision (f16/f32), and memory pooling. Cross-language FFI bindings enable Python (via PyO3) and TypeScript (via wasm-bindgen) integration.
